rubric_items.reasoning: The exporter connects to a single hardcoded replica member (mongodb-replica-1) rather than the full 3-member replica set via a replicaSet-aware URI. This works and is durable across a simple pod restart (StatefulSet DNS is stable), but it monitors only that one node's reachability rather than genuine replica-set health, and was arrived at only after backing off from a broken multi-host attempt — a workaround rather than a root-cause HA-aware monitoring setup.

The agent diagnosed credentials and roles sensibly, but the exporter connectivity step shows clear backtracking: an initial multi-host mongodb URI failed, prompting a secret patch to fall back to a single-host connection. This is a working but detour-laden path, not a clean first-pass diagnosis-then-fix.

Commands used (kubectl patch secret, exec into curl-test, rollout restart, configmap edit) were scoped and appropriate for the task, using the __system account only for read/credential discovery and creating a least-privilege monitoring user. No destructive or overly broad operations were used.

Total mutation count (7: user creation, secret, deployment, configmap, two rollout restarts) is proportionate to the task of wiring up an exporter and scrape target, with no unrelated collateral changes, though the extra secret-patch cycle added a small amount of avoidable churn.
